title: Here’s what Patriots could reportedly net in a Kayshon Boutte trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots wide receiver Kayshon Boutte might be traded following A.J. Brown's anticipated arrival.
Boutte, who caught 33 passes for 551 yards last season, is absent from Patriots' voluntary workouts.
Sports Illustrated reports Boutte might be traded for a fifth- or sixth-round draft pick.

title: Fundraisers launched as 8 remain hospitalized days after fire, explosion at Maine lumber mill
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A fire and explosion at Robbins Lumber mill in Searsmont, Maine, killed firefighter Andrew Cross and injured 12 others.
Over $30,000 raised for first responders after Maine mill explosion; the GoFundMe aims for a $40,000 goal.
Investigators face difficult conditions; Maine's State Fire Marshal and ATF are examining the incident alongside mill cooperation.

title: Shams Charania expects Celtics’ pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo to carry into ‘this offseason’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics are exploring a potential trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo amid the Bucks' openness to offers.
Giannis Antetokounmpo averaged 30.4 points, 11.9 rebounds, and 6.5 assists in the 2024-25 NBA season.
The Celtics face competition from teams like the Heat, Lakers, and Timberwolves in pursuing Antetokounmpo.

title: T police: Man’s throat slashed in bus station altercation
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man's throat was slashed during an altercation at the MBTA Nubian Square bus station Friday night.
The victim, whose injury was non-life threatening, was taken to a local hospital after receiving immediate aid from Transit Police.
Police are investigating the incident and have not confirmed if the suspect has been arrested.

title: Red Sox get discouraging update on Roman Anthony’s injury after he tries swinging bat
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony's return to the Red Sox is delayed due to soreness from a sprained ligament in his right ring finger.
Interim manager Chad Tracy announced Anthony will be sidelined from baseball activities until at least Thursday for rest.
Initially expected to return by May 15, Anthony's recent setback may push his return to the upcoming series against the Twins.

title: Bill Belichick gets candid on Tom Brady’s growth with Patriots: ‘He wasn’t even really good, but he became great’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick discussed Tom Brady's early career on 'Pardon My Take,' highlighting Brady's growth from fourth-string quarterback to star.
Brady was initially a game manager at the Patriots, evolving into a crucial player by the 2003-2004 seasons, according to Belichick.
Belichick expressed enjoyment in his role as UNC head coach despite the team's 4-8 record, highlighting the coaching opportunities it provides.

title: As Minogue pours millions into his own campaign, state lawmaker pushes to ban self-funding
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: GOP candidate Mike Minogue has contributed $13.5 million to his own gubernatorial campaign in Massachusetts.
State Sen. Rebecca Rausch proposes a budget amendment to ban unlimited self-funding of political campaigns in Massachusetts.
Rausch's amendment seeks to close the loophole allowing candidates unlimited self-funding, highlighting broader concerns about democracy.

title: Celtics fans voted NBA’s ‘most obnoxious’ in anonymous player poll
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics fans were voted the NBA's 'most obnoxious' in an anonymous player poll conducted by The Athletic.
TD Garden was rated as having the most passionate fans, narrowly beating Madison Square Garden with 20.3% of the vote.
In predictions about Giannis Antetokounmpo's offseason destination, the Celtics were not among the top eight teams mentioned.

title: Stephen A. Smith responds to Jaylen Brown’s comments: ‘Be careful what you wish for’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephen A. Smith responds to Jaylen Brown's criticism, warning him to be cautious with his media statements.
Brown labeled Smith as the 'face of clickbait media', prompting Smith to caution him about potential backlash.
Smith suggests Brown reflect on his actions after controversial comments following the Celtics' playoff elimination.

title: FAA awarding Logan Airport $2.8 million for family-friendly upgrades
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Logan Airport receives $2.8 million from the FAA for renovating Kidports play areas in Terminals A, B, and C.
The funding is part of $970 million allocated for family-friendly airport improvements through the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
The upgrades aim to provide child-friendly spaces and enhance the travel experience for families, according to U.S. Department of Transportation.
